get_running_config:router2
get_running_config:router3

root_causes.cause: R2's DHCP pool for the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN is handing out the wrong default gateway (192.168.0.1) so hosts (e.g., 172.16.0.12) will use an unreachable/incorrect next-hop and replies to remote pings will be sent to the wrong router.
evidence: Alert: Host 172.16.0.12 unreachable (0/25 ICMP pings).
get_dhcp_config:router2 (R2_LAN) -> default-router list: ["192.168.0.1"] (should be an address on 172.16.0.0/24).
get_running_config:router2 -> GigabitEthernet0/0/0 address: 172.16.0.1/24 (R2's LAN gateway IP is 172.16.0.1).
get_running_config:router2 and get_ospf:router2 -> OSPF is advertising 172.16.0.0/24 from R2 (so network presence and inter-router routing are functioning).
get_running_config:router2 -> DHCP excluded-address list contains 172.16.0.1-172.16.0.10, indicating DHCP is intended to serve the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN.

data.intent_description: Host 172.16.0.12 is unreachable because R2's DHCP pool is handing out the wrong default gateway (192.168.0.1). Desired outcome: update DHCP on R2 so hosts receive 172.16.0.1 as their default gateway and restore reachability.

plan.problem: DHCP pool R2_LAN on router2 has default-router set to 192.168.0.1, causing 172.16.0.0/24 hosts (e.g., 172.16.0.12) to use an incorrect/unreachable gateway.
plan.fix_summary: Change DHCP pool R2_LAN on router2 to set default-router to 172.16.0.1.

evidence: Verification get_dhcp_config (call_jeHfResS0kuC4XznW6ki5VeX) shows router2 DHCP pool R2_LAN default-router-list: ["172.16.0.1"] (problematic gateway replaced).
Verification output also shows router2 DHCP excluded-address low-high 172.16.0.1-172.16.0.10 (call_jeHfResS0kuC4XznW6ki5VeX), consistent with the 172.16.0.0/24 pool.
Earlier snapshot get_dhcp_config:router2 (call_nHy0EZo96skNO40NTrynBpU2) showed the incorrect default-router "192.168.0.1", confirming the misconfiguration was present and has since been corrected.

sources.name: get_running_config:router1
get_running_config:router2
get_running_config:router3

root_causes.cause: R2's DHCP pool for the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN is handing out the wrong default gateway (192.168.0.1) so hosts (e.g., 172.16.0.12) will use an unreachable/incorrect next-hop and replies to remote pings will be sent to the wrong router.
evidence: Alert: Host 172.16.0.12 unreachable (0/25 ICMP pings).
get_dhcp_config:router2 (R2_LAN) -> default-router list: ["192.168.0.1"] (should be an address on 172.16.0.0/24).
get_running_config:router2 -> GigabitEthernet0/0/0 address: 172.16.0.1/24 (R2's LAN gateway IP is 172.16.0.1).
get_running_config:router2 and get_ospf:router2 -> OSPF is advertising 172.16.0.0/24 from R2 (so network presence and inter-router routing are functioning).
get_running_config:router2 -> DHCP excluded-address list contains 172.16.0.1-172.16.0.10, indicating DHCP is intended to serve the 172.16.0.0/24 LAN.

plan.problem: DHCP pool R2_LAN on router2 has default-router set to 192.168.0.1, causing 172.16.0.0/24 hosts (e.g., 172.16.0.12) to use an incorrect/unreachable gateway.
plan.fix_summary: Change DHCP pool R2_LAN on router2 to set default-router to 172.16.0.1.

evidence: Verification get_dhcp_config (call_jeHfResS0kuC4XznW6ki5VeX) shows router2 DHCP pool R2_LAN default-router-list: ["172.16.0.1"] (problematic gateway replaced).
Verification output also shows router2 DHCP excluded-address low-high 172.16.0.1-172.16.0.10 (call_jeHfResS0kuC4XznW6ki5VeX), consistent with the 172.16.0.0/24 pool.
Earlier snapshot get_dhcp_config:router2 (call_nHy0EZo96skNO40NTrynBpU2) showed the incorrect default-router "192.168.0.1", confirming the misconfiguration was present and has since been corrected.

messages.content:
What was observed
- Host 172.16.0.12 was unreachable (0/25 ICMP replies).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- R2's DHCP pool R2_LAN was handing out the wrong default gateway (192.168.0.1) instead of R2's LAN IP, so hosts on 172.16.0.0/24 used an incorrect next hop.

Changes made
- Updated R2 DHCP pool R2_LAN: set default-router to 172.16.0.1 (recreated/updated the pool).

Verification
- Passed. get_dhcp_config on R2 now shows R2_LAN default-router-list: ["172.16.0.1"] and excluded-address 172.16.0.1–172.16.0.10. No remaining issues detected.

What was observed
- Host 172.16.0.12 was unreachable (0/25 ICMP replies).

Diagnosis (main cause)
- R2's DHCP pool R2_LAN was handing out the wrong default gateway (192.168.0.1) instead of R2's LAN IP, so hosts on 172.16.0.0/24 used an incorrect next hop.

Changes made
- Updated R2 DHCP pool R2_LAN: set default-router to 172.16.0.1 (recreated/updated the pool).

Verification
- Passed. get_dhcp_config on R2 now shows R2_LAN default-router-list: ["172.16.0.1"] and excluded-address 172.16.0.1–172.16.0.10. No remaining issues detected.
